UAE VAT Calculator

Add or extract 5% VAT instantly

Calculate UAE Value Added Tax on any amount. Switch between adding VAT to a net amount or extracting VAT from an inclusive price.

AED
AED 1AED 1,000,000
Net Amount (excl. VAT)AED 1,000
VAT Amount (5%)AED 50
Total Amount (incl. VAT)AED 1,050

UAE VAT Overview

The UAE introduced Value Added Tax (VAT) at a standard rate of 5% on January 1, 2018. It applies to most goods and services, with some exemptions (healthcare, education, certain financial services, residential property).

Businesses with taxable supplies exceeding AED 375,000 must register for VAT. Voluntary registration is available for businesses exceeding AED 187,500.

VAT-Exempt and Zero-Rated Supplies

  • Zero-rated (0%): Exports, international transport, certain precious metals, first sale of residential property (within 3 years)
  • Exempt: Certain financial services, residential property (rent and resale), bare land, local passenger transport
  • Standard (5%): Everything else — restaurants, electronics, clothing, services, commercial property

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I calculate VAT from a total price?
To extract VAT from an inclusive price: divide the total by 1.05 to get the net amount, then subtract. For example: AED 1,050 / 1.05 = AED 1,000 (net) and AED 50 (VAT). Use the "Extract VAT" mode above for instant calculation.
Do tourists get VAT refunds in UAE?
Yes. Tourists can claim VAT refunds on eligible purchases of AED 250+ per transaction through the Tax Refund for Tourists Scheme operated by Planet. Refunds are processed at designated points in airports.
Is rent subject to VAT in UAE?
Residential property rent is exempt from VAT. Commercial property rent is subject to 5% VAT. Hotel accommodation is taxable at 5% VAT plus tourism fees.
